heading: Step 3: Run the  method to create the index.
body:
**Input:**

```shell
db.<collection>.createSearchIndex(
  "default",
  {
    "mappings": {
      "dynamic": false,
      "fields": {
        "<field-name>": {
          "type": "geo",
          "indexShapes": true|false
        }
      }
    }
  }
)
```

**Output:**

```
default
```

The MongoDB Search `geo` type takes the following parameters:

| Option      | Type    | Necessity | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Default |
| ----------- | ------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- |
| type        | string  | Required  | Human-readable label that identifies this field type. UI value must be Geo and JSON (Javascript Object Notation) value must be geo.                                                                                                       |         |
| indexShapes | boolean | Optional  | Flag that indicates whether to index shapes. By default, MongoDB Search:Indexes points, even when nested.Doesn't index shape geometries such as lines and polygons.Value can be:true to index shapes and pointsfalse to index only points | false   |

The following index definition examples use collections in the
[sample data sets](available-sample-datasets). After you
[Load Sample Data Into Atlas](load-sample-data), you can use
these examples to index fields as the dateFacet type.

To learn how to set up and run the following example code, see
Define the Index for the token Type.

The following index definition for the `sample_airbnb.listingsAndReviews` collection
indexes the `address.location` field as the `geo` type. This index allows
you to query against that field by using the MongoDB Search [geoShape](geoshape-ref)
and [geoWithin](geowithin-ref) operators.

```javascript
db.listingsAndReviews.createSearchIndex(
    "default",
    {
    "mappings": { 
        "dynamic": false,
        "fields": {
            "address": {
                "fields" : {
                    "location": {
                        "type": "geo",
                        "indexShapes": true
                    } 
                }
            }
        }
    }}
)
```

To learn more about the operators you can use to query the `geo` type, see the following operator reference pages:

- [geoShape](geoshape-ref)

- [geoWithin](geowithin-ref)

You can use the MongoDB Search `geo` type to index geographic points and shape
coordinates. For this type, the indexed field must be a [GeoJSON](manual/reference/geojson) object. You can use the [geoShape](geoshape-ref) and
[geoWithin](geowithin-ref) operators to query indexed fields of type `geo`.

dateFacet type is not included in the dynamic mapping by default. To index fields
as dateFacet type, you can use [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings) or configure a `typeSet` to include dateFacet in dynamic mapping.

Choose your preferred configuration method in the Atlas UI and then select the database and collection.

Visual Editor

- Click Refine Your Index to configure your index.

- In the Field Mappings section, click Add
  Field Mapping to open the Add Field Mapping window.

- Click Customized Configuration.

- Select the field to index from the Field Name dropdown.

  > **Note:** You can't index fields that contain the dollar (`$`) sign at the
  > start of the field name.

- Click the Data Type dropdown and select
  UUID.

- Configure the field properties for the dateFacet type. To
  learn more, see Field Properties.

- Click Add.

JSON Editor

The following is the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) syntax for the dateFacet type.
Replace the default index definition with the following. To learn more
about the fields, see Field Properties.

```json
{
  "mappings": {
    "dynamic": true|false,
    "fields": {
      "<field-name>": {
        "type": "geo",
        "indexShapes": true|false
      }
    }
  }
}
```

The MongoDB Search `geo` type takes the following parameters:

| Option      | Type    | Necessity | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Default |
| ----------- | ------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- |
| type        | string  | Required  | Human-readable label that identifies this field type. UI value must be Geo and JSON (Javascript Object Notation) value must be geo.                                                                                                       |         |
| indexShapes | boolean | Optional  | Flag that indicates whether to index shapes. By default, MongoDB Search:Indexes points, even when nested.Doesn't index shape geometries such as lines and polygons.Value can be:true to index shapes and pointsfalse to index only points | false   |

The following index definition examples use collections in the
[sample data sets](available-sample-datasets). After you
[Load Sample Data Into Atlas](load-sample-data), you can use
these examples to index fields as the dateFacet type.

To learn how to set up and run the following example code, see
Define the Index for the token Type.

The following index definition for the `sample_airbnb.listingsAndReviews` collection
indexes the `address.location` field as the `geo` type. This index allows
you to query against that field by using the MongoDB Search [geoShape](geoshape-ref)
and [geoWithin](geowithin-ref) operators.

Visual Editor

- In the Add Field Mapping window, select
  address.location from the Field
  Name dropdown.

- Click the Data Type dropdown and select
  Geo.

- Modify the Geo Properties to set the value for
  Index Shapes to `true`.

- Click Add.

JSON Editor

Replace the default index definition with the following
definition:

```json
{
   "mappings": {
      "dynamic": false,
      "fields": {
         "address": {
            "type": "document",
            "fields": {
               "location": {
                  "type": "geo",
                  "indexShapes": true
               }
            }
         }
      }
   }
}
```

To learn more about the operators you can use to query the `geo` type, see the following operator reference pages:

- [geoShape](geoshape-ref)

- [geoWithin](geowithin-ref)

You can use the MongoDB Search `geo` type to index geographic points and shape
coordinates. For this type, the indexed field must be a [GeoJSON](manual/reference/geojson) object. You can use the [geoShape](geoshape-ref) and
[geoWithin](geowithin-ref) operators to query indexed fields of type `geo`.

dateFacet type is not included in the dynamic mapping by default. To index fields
as dateFacet type, you can use [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings) or configure a `typeSet` to include dateFacet in dynamic mapping.
